title: Johnson & Johnson Financial Summary From 2025 Form 10-K
text: Johnson & Johnson's 2025 Form 10-K reported worldwide sales to customers of $94.2 billion, up 6.0% from $88.8 billion in 2024. Innovative Medicine generated $60.4 billion of sales, while MedTech generated $33.8 billion. Total income before tax was $32.6 billion in 2025, compared with $16.7 billion in 2024, helped partly by a reversal of a significant portion of a previously accrued talc reserve. The company reported identifiable assets of $199.2 billion at year-end 2025. J&J's sales base is diversified across U.S. and international markets, with U.S. sales of $53.8 billion and international sales of $40.4 billion. The financial profile shows a large, diversified healthcare company with strong sales growth in Innovative Medicine and MedTech. Key risks include patent expirations, biosimilar competition, legal proceedings, pricing pressure, clinical trial execution, regulatory decisions, and the need to continue replacing older product revenue with new medicines and technologies.
